programme.title: Diagnosis Murder

programme.title: Diagnosis Murder
programme.desc: A councillor's wife is found dead in suspicious circumstances and all the evidence points to her husband - but Steve believes the man is being framed by corrupt policemen. Medical detective drama, starring Dick Van Dyke

programme.title: The Fugitive

programme.title: Perry Mason

programme.title: Walker, Texas Ranger

programme.title: CSI: Miami
programme.desc: A suspected carjacker is murdered and the team believes he may have been involved in drug smuggling. To complicate matters, evidence suggests that Ryan, who is now working for local TV, could have inadvertently led the killer to his victim, a possibility that further distances him from his former colleagues. Crime drama, starring David Caruso

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: Part two of two. DiNozzo, McGee and the FBI continue to track political connections to a case, and Bishop sends DiNozzo a souvenir. Meanwhile, Gibbs and Special Agent Pride chase leads in New Orleans after evidence points to a copycat killer. Starring Mark Harmon, David McCallum, Pauley Perrette and Scott Bakula

programme.title: NCIS: Los Angeles
programme.desc: Marine gunnery sergeant Wendell Dobbs is gunned down in a drive-by shooting by assailants wielding AK-47s, and the team suspects the victim may have been involved in illegally importing automatic weapons into the country and supplying them to local gangs. Sam goes undercover to find the truth after learning Dobbs gave up his combat post to mentor his younger brother, who had been hanging out with a gang. Crime drama, starring Chris O'Donnell

programme.title: The Fugitive

programme.desc: Tony and Ziva track a suspected terrorist to a warehouse, where they find traces of radioactive metal - together with evidence suggesting a nuclear attack is being planned on Washington, DC. programme.title: NCIS: Los Angeles
programme.desc: Marine Daniel Zuna is found dead in a pool of his own blood in an alleyway outside a night club, and the team's first stop is to investigate the mixed martial arts gym where the victim spent most of his off-duty time. Kensi poses as Zuna's new girlfriend and suspects the murder could be related to something that happened in Iraq, prompting Sam to go undercover as an MMA fighter in order to see if he can find out the truth. Crime drama, starring LL Cool J and Daniela Ruah

programme.title: Casualty
programme.desc: Part one of two. The mobile medical team treats patients in the church hall, while Lance and Tess respond to a baby's cry among the debris, but fall into the mineshaft below

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Part one of two. Disgraced detective Zain Nadir is interviewed about drug trafficking in Hayes End Prison, where he is serving 10 years for perverting the course of justice, and agrees to help his colleagues infiltrate the gang involved. Jack is not sure whether he can trust his mole and fears the worst when a riot breaks out just as the police move in to arrest the operation's kingpin, Zain's cellmate Jason Forbes, who makes good his escape in the confusion. TJ Ramini and Lloyd McGuire guest star

programme.title: Classic EastEnders

programme.title: Classic EastEnders
programme.desc: Dan plays a dangerous joke on Sarah with a tab of Ecstasy. Bianca advises Tiffany about Tony. Tony, meanwhile, meets up with Simon who takes him to a gay bar

programme.title: Soldier, Soldier
programme.desc: B Company's new commander is unimpressed by his men's performances, and Dave's personal problems take their toll

programme.title: All Creatures Great and Small
programme.desc: James discovers the downside of life as a vet when the post-mortem results for Lord Hulton's horse arrive. Siegfried's younger brother Tristan comes back from college and James is introduced to Mrs Pumphrey and her dog Tricki Woo

programme.title: Lovejoy
programme.desc: The roguish antiques dealer is arrested on suspicion of armed robbery - and to make matters worse, the only way he can clear his name is by admitting he was with a charming ex-girlfriend at the time. He must decide whether to come clean and face a jealous Charlotte's wrath, or take the consequences of the charge. Ian McShane stars

programme.title: Brush Strokes
programme.desc: Jacko and Sandra go on honeymoon to Barbados after their failed wedding - and decide not to get married after all. However, they neglect to tell their friends about their decision

programme.title: Are You Being Served?
programme.desc: Mrs Slocombe takes charge of the department while Mr Rumbold is off sick, leading to friction with the staff. Shop-floor comedy, with John Inman, Mollie Sugden and Frank Thornton

programme.title: Last of the Summer Wine
programme.desc: Foggy, Compo and Clegg spot Digby chasing his donkey around his paddock. Foggy claims that a little planning will see the beast caught, and volunteers the trio to complete the task. Brian Wilde, Peter Sallis and Bill Owen star

programme.title: Last of the Summer Wine
programme.desc: Compo takes up marathon running, hoping that it will persuade Nora Batty to fall into his arms. Long-running rural comedy, starring Bill Owen and Brian Wilde

programme.title: The Inspector Lynley Mysteries
programme.desc: Feature-length episode. Havers is sent to Buckinghamshire to investigate the death of a radical left-wing politician's illegitimate daughter, whose body has been found in a river. Teaming up with a friendly and helpful local police constable, she makes good headway with the case - until the disappearance of a second child complicates matters. Nathaniel Parker, Sharon Small and Lesley Vickerage star

programme.title: New Tricks
programme.desc: The body of a missing computer expert turns up in a hospital morgue under a false name, so the team reopens the investigation into his disappearance. The dead man worked for the Metropolitan Police, giving him access to a great deal of sensitive information - added to which, he was in contact with a group of online hackers. But he died of natural causes - so why was his body hidden, and by who? As the seasoned detectives unravel the mystery, Brian struggles to accept Steve as the new Ucos member, forcing Esther to intervene. Guest starring Sarah Smart and Alex Jennings

programme.title: Waking the Dead
programme.desc: Feature-length episode. A woman who went missing from a care home in 1986 is found murdered in a burnt-out car, and the team discovers she had recently abducted a teenage girl herself. As the investigation continues, both cases appear to be linked to an urban myth about a character known as the Bag Man - an eyeless villain who kidnaps children. With Eva Birthistle, Tara FitzGerald and Wil Johnson

programme.title: The Heart Guy
programme.desc: Hugh and the hospital staff use blackmail to get rid of their incompetent boss Norah, while Penny faces family and romantic dilemmas that will change her future

programme.title: Mercy Street
programme.desc: A house guest becomes the focus of Alice's schemes. When Mary falls gravely ill, Foster's attempts to care for her antagonise the new chief. Charlotte tries to contain the smallpox epidemic and Samuel has to make a difficult decision.

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Part two of two. Zain gets a frosty reception at the station when Heaton secures his release from prison to help with the recapture of escaped killer Jason Forbes, and Will can't help taking a swing at the man who callously disposed of Honey Harman's body in a bid to save his own skin. The pursuit leads to a gunpoint showdown, giving Zain a clear choice between escape and doing the right thing. TJ Ramini guest stars

programme.desc: Tiffany decides to keep her baby and to try to make things work with Tony. Meanwhile, Tony admits to Simon that he's gay

programme.title: Classic EastEnders
programme.desc: Back from Blackpool, Simon and Tiffany have questions for Tony. Lorraine finds a solution to a problem of Grant's. Cindy seeks advice, while Ian pursues his suspicions

programme.title: Lovejoy
programme.desc: A penniless sculptor seeks the canny dealer's help to prise a considerable inheritance out of his avaricious brother. Celia Imrie guest stars, with Ian McShane

programme.title: Brush Strokes
programme.desc: Lionel becomes suspicious about Jacko and Sandra deciding to call off the wedding. Lesley considers getting back together with Giles, while Gloria has her own eye on Jacko

programme.title: Are You Being Served?
programme.desc: Captain Peacock is far from happy when he realises that the head of the sports department has spread the news about a painful boil on his bottom - and eventually defends his honour in a boxing match. Comedy, starring Frank Thornton

programme.title: Last of the Summer Wine
programme.desc: Ivy goes out for the afternoon - leaving Foggy to supervise Crusher and ensure the cafe runs smoothly. Comedy, starring Brian Wilde, Jane Freeman and Jonathan Linsley

programme.title: Last of the Summer Wine
programme.desc: Howard asks a reluctant Clegg to deliver a message to Marina at her place of employment. Meanwhile, Seymour goes in search of oil, but manages to drill through a water main. Comedy, starring Peter Sallis and Michael Aldridge

programme.title: Judge John Deed
programme.desc: While presiding over a murder trial in which a lawyer is representing himself and his younger siblings, Deed rekindles his affair with the scheming Francesca Rochester, ignoring his pregnant daughter's pleas for help in the process. Blind to what's happening around him, and resisting warnings from Jo, the judge eventually realises his mistress has been deceiving him and must hope he has not left it too late to come to the aid of Charlie and her unborn child. High Court drama, starring Martin Shaw, Jenny Seagrove and Billie Whitelaw

programme.title: New Tricks
programme.desc: The death of a teenage tennis star occupies the team when it is suggested her suicide jump two years earlier was anything but. They soon find several people with motives for her murder, including her coach Nick and agent Anthony, but it seems the one with the most to benefit from the girl's demise was her great rival Fawn Brammall - a shy and subdued player who proves difficult to question. Meanwhile, Gerry wonders if he did enough to encourage his own daughter's sporting ambitions and Brian hatches a plan to turn his dog into a movie star. Detective drama, guest starring Tamzin Outhwaite and Alexei Sayle

programme.title: Waking the Dead
programme.desc: Feature-length episode. The body of a Falklands War veteran who became one of the leading lights in the campaign against nuclear weapons is found in a stretch of river running underneath the Ministry of Defence. As Boyd and the team set about investigating the man's former allies, they stumble upon footage of an incident intelligence services had tried to cover up - and Grace is reunited with a former lover from her own time in the anti-nuclear movement. Drama, starring Trevor Eve and Sue Johnston, with Jack Shepherd (Wycliffe, Into the Storm), John McArdle (Brookside) and Anna Chancellor (Four Weddings and a Funeral, Spooks)

programme.title: The Heart Guy
programme.desc: Hugh creates havoc for Charlie when he reveals her secret talent. Penny's first day back in Whyhope is headed for disaster until she joins forces with Hugh

programme.title: Mercy Street
programme.desc: As Pinkerton looks at the Greens more closely, they join forces to hide an ugly secret. McBurney, hoping to distance Foster from Mary, sends him to a nearby army camp on a house call. Alice helps Frank escape and Samuel earns Charlotte's respect

programme.title: Casualty
programme.desc: Tess reaches breaking point as Sam becomes increasingly distracted by the wayward influence of Fleur, who persuades him to sell his tablets to her friends and take the afternoon off work. Guppy begs Harry to let Jas resign quietly rather than be reported to the GMC, and Woody finds his past catching up with him when he begins a friendship with a 10-year-old patient. Medical drama, starring Luke Bailey

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: In the first of a two-part story, new recruit DS Max Carter (Christopher Fox) joins the Sun Hill team from CO19 to investigate a tip-off about an armed robbery, only to find partner Terry drowning his sorrows after the informant is killed for his pains. Sally and Ben arrest two girls for failing to pay their cab fare, and a catastrophic train of events is unleashed when it emerges they were carrying a deadly cargo smuggled in from Ghana - and their boyfriends want it back

programme.title: Classic EastEnders
programme.desc: A face from the past turns up to surprise David and Cindy. Frankie talks herself into a job and there's an unwanted guest at Tiffany's birthday party

programme.title: Classic EastEnders
programme.desc: Nigel offers to help Lorraine. Robbie finds buried treasure. Ian is presented with the evidence of Cindy's adultery with David Wicks

programme.title: Soldier, Soldier
programme.desc: B Company are on exercise in the Northern Territory of Australia, and their skills are put to the test during a trip in the outback

programme.title: All Creatures Great and Small
programme.desc: James realises he has a rival for Helen's affections, while Siegfried reaches breaking point with Miss Harbottle. Tristan has another all-nighter with the bell ringers, leading Siegfried to believe he has too much time on his hands - so he puts him in charge of some newly acquired piglets

programme.title: Lovejoy
programme.desc: The police suspect Lovejoy of stealing from one of Jane's old friends, forcing him to enlist the help of refuse collector extraordinaire Brian Nun to clear himself. Unfortunately, matters are complicated by Eric's 'bin diving' habit. Light-hearted drama, starring Ian McShane and Phyllis Logan

programme.title: Brush Strokes
programme.desc: A newly single Jacko considers what he wants to do with his life - only to be reunited with his resentful brother Reg. Guest starring Kenneth MacDonald (Only Fools and Horses)

programme.title: Are You Being Served?
programme.desc: After complaining about their long wait for lunch in the canteen, the staff are told to take over the kitchens themselves - but when they arrive for their shift, they find the area unhygienic. Comedy, starring Mollie Sugden, John Inman and Frank Thornton

programme.title: Last of the Summer Wine
programme.desc: Seymour takes it upon himself to supervise repairs to a boat, and together with Clegg tries to persuade Compo to take up canoeing to improve his physique. Comedy, starring Michael Aldridge, Peter Sallis and Bill Owen

programme.title: Last of the Summer Wine
programme.desc: Compo accidently discards something of sentimental value, and waxes lyrical about the two subjects closest to his heart - love and ferrets. Meanwhile, Seymour demonstrates his new home security device. Long-running rural comedy, starring Bill Owen and Michael Aldridge

programme.title: Dalziel & Pascoe
programme.desc: The discovery of a man's body in a disused mine shaft reawakens villagers' suspicions over a girl's death - and the mismatched duo's investigation lifts the lid on murder, mystery and intrigue. Feature-length drama, starring Warren Clarke and Colin Buchanan as the chalk-and-cheese detectives, with Polly Hemingway and Joe Duttine

programme.title: New Tricks
programme.desc: Sandra and the team unearth the 10-year-old case of a poet from Belfast whose burnt body was found in the scrapyard of a known gangster. Gerry becomes convinced the victim's links with the criminal fraternity led to his demise, but Brian has a different theory, believing the secret of his murder lies in his verse. Amanda Redman, Dennis Waterman and Alun Armstrong star

programme.title: Waking the Dead
programme.desc: Feature-length episode. The team discovers a DNA match between a medical student reported missing six years previously and a corpse that was found at around the same time. Investigations into the man's background reveal he had been suspected of having links to terrorist organisations - but Boyd believes a high-level cover-up is obscuring the whole truth about the case. Guest starring Don Warrington

As if that were not enough, they have to guess a breed of dog while blindfolded, before one last dogwalk parade. Who will impress judges Verity Hardcastle and Colin Taylor the most?

programme.title: For Love or Money

programme.title: Cornwall: This Fishing Life
programme.desc: Life in Cadgwith Cove, which is Cornwall's last traditional full-time fishing cove. For generations, sons have followed fathers off the beach and out to sea, and now the next generation is set to join the fleet. But while there might be new blood, the old working fabric of the cove itself is now under threat

programme.desc: Study tips, with Cel Spellman

programme.title: Bang Goes the Theory
programme.desc: The team explores the technological revolution arising from the world of so-called 'big data' - computers' ability to store and analyse unimaginable amounts of information. Liz Bonnin reveals how the technology behind the computerised monitoring of Rolls-Royce jet engines could be used to detect brain injuries in critically ill patients, and Jem Stansfield builds his own low-tech storage system, using hacksaw blades and magnets. Maggie Philbin explores the darker side of data collection, asking how much personal information is out there - and how easy it is to find

programme.title: The Nine to Five with Stacey Dooley

programme.title: A to Z of TV Gardening
programme.desc: Carol Kirkwood looks at subjects beginning with the letter S, continuing her alphabetical journey through the world of horticulture. Along the way, she gets inspiration and advice from some of the BBC's most popular gardening presenters

programme.title: Put Your Money Where Your Mouth Is
programme.desc: Eric Knowles takes on Chuko Ojiri at an antiques fair in Somerset, with the two taking very different approaches to spending their £750 budgets

programme.title: The Great Rift: Africa's Wild Heart
programme.desc: How the diverse array of waterways in East Africa's Great Rift Valley - including rivers, coral seas, caustic springs and waterfalls - helps to sustain the region's wildlife. A wide variety of birds, animals and fish rely on the water for survival, and many have evolved to adapt to the challenges posed by the area's geography

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: Will Axon and Mark Stacey examine a telescope that once belonged to a naval captain, and a rare Watcombe clock, as they appraise items brought Powderham Castle in Devon

programme.title: Inside the Zoo
programme.desc: Vets at Edinburgh Zoo deal with a zebra with arthritis, while a binturong is being prepared to leave for the south of France as part of a breeding programme, and keepers Vickie and Laura try to weigh a polar bear

programme.title: Interior Design Masters
programme.desc: The designers work alone for the first time in the contest, each being assigned to transform a beach hut at Walton-on-the-Naze in Essex, in just two days on a budget of £1000. Working in a small space presents a serious challenge, and while some designers decide to incorporate existing features in their vision, others ruthlessly strip the huts bare in a bid to make their mark. Helping Michelle Ogundehin judge the results is journalist and interior designer Sophie Robinson

programme.title: DNA Family Secrets
programme.desc: Stacey Dooley helps people answer life-changing questions by examining their DNA. In the first episode, she comes to the aid of Richard, who wants to learn if a man claiming to be his biological father is the genuine article, and Charlie, who wants to know what her chances are of contracting the same terminal illness as her father

programme.title: The Ranganation
programme.desc: Romesh Ranganathan hosts a topical comedy show, joined virtually by a diverse group of 20 members of the public drawn from all walks of life, who are invited to share their thoughts on the state of modern Britain today. Romesh and his outspoken and passionate focus group will be joined by special guests Joe Lycett and Judi Love, who will be beamed into the studio to chew over the lighter end of the news spectrum

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Analysis of the day's events, presented by Emily Maitlis

programme.title: Chris Packham's Animal Einsteins
programme.desc: The presenter introduces a look at animals with impressive communication skills, discovering how white-throated sparrows use song to attract mates and the complex vocabulary of prairie dogs. The programme reveals how dolphins are able to address each other by name and chimpanzees use sign language, and also reveals species that communicate using ultrasound, vibrations and parts of the light spectrum beyond the ability of human sense to process

programme.title: Lily: A Transgender Story
programme.desc: Filmed over five years, cameras follow 20-year-old Lily Jones, as she transitions from male to female. During this period she leaves her seaside home in Aberystwyth for a new life in Birmingham, undergoes gender reassignment surgery and finds love

programme.title: Your Garden Made Perfect
programme.desc: Pam and Marcus have a big garden surrounded by large established trees, which stretches away from their suburban home in St Albans, Hertfordshire. Now their children have grown up, they want to transform the garden into a space they they can enjoy themselves. Designer Helen Elks-Smith is set on a design that begins with the home, while rival Tom Massey wants to transport them to another world. Which idea will the couple choose? Presented by Angela Scanlon

programme.title: Amazing Hotels: Life Beyond the Lobby
programme.desc: Monica Galetti and Giles Coren visit the Yorkshire Dales to work at Swinton Estate, the ancestral home of Lord and Lady Masham. By taking a look the heart of the grand house and its countryside charm, as well as the wider estate, which has opened up to for guests to enjoy, Monica and Giles learn about the challenges of making history pay in the modern world

programme.desc: In light of suggestions of 'truth warnings' for The Crown, and heated debate about colour-blind casting in Bridgerton, Mary talks to Line of Duty creator Jed Mercurio about how much fiction is needed to make fact into a hit TV drama. She also debates the perils of straddling the fact-fiction divide with writers James Graham and Simon Jenkins, and examines why this year's awards season features so many movies based on real-life events

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: At Ushaw in Co Durham, antique experts David Fletcher and James Lewis put a value on the region's antiques and collectibles. David is delighted by a 1950s toy plane, while James finds a 1940s menu from a famous Hollywood restaurant which has been signed by some top film stars. Host Paul Martin visits the Mining Art Gallery in Bishop Auckland to find out why many coal miners were inspired to pick up a paintbrush

programme.title: Inside the Zoo

programme.title: My Hero
programme.desc: Occasional programme in which celebrities explore the lives of those who have influenced them, beginning with Miranda Hart paying tribute to Eric Morecambe. She tells his story by retracing his steps, visiting the places he appeared with partner Ernie Wise, meeting people who knew him and introducing the performances that made her want to enter the comedy world. The journey takes her across Britain, from Wales - where she talks to writer Eddie Braben, the man behind many of Eric and Ernie's most famous TV moments - to the Essex studio of the artist who painted the entertainer at the height of his fame

programme.title: The Terror
programme.desc: Thriller anthology series inspired by infamous or mysterious real-life historical tragedies. In the first episode, the crew of a 19th century Royal Naval expedition are sent on a dangerous mission to find the Arctic's treacherous Northwest Passage. Starring Ciaran Hinds, Jared Harris, Tobias Menzies and Adam Nagaitis

programme.title: The Terror
programme.desc: After a long winter trapped in the ice, scouting parties are sent out in search of open water. The past tensions between Sir John and Crozier are made worse by the dangerous situation they find themselves in. One of the teams accidentally shoots an Inuit man after mistaking him for a polar bear, but as they race to his aid they make a terrifying discovery

programme.title: Some People with Jokes
programme.desc: A selection of people from around Britain are joined by their beloved pooches while they share favourite gags with the viewing public, with everything from one-liners to shaggy dog stories proving it's not just their four-legged friends that are barking

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Analysis of the day's events, with Emily Maitlis

programme.title: Chris Packham: Asperger's and Me
programme.desc: The naturalist and broadcaster offers a personal and brutally honest account of his life coping with Asperger's syndrome, reflecting on the devastating struggles of his adolescence. With scientific advances offering new possibilities to treat his condition, he travels to America to witness radical therapies that appear to offer the chance of entirely eradicating problematic autistic traits, while his long-term partner Charlotte talks about the problems Asperger's creates in their relationship

programme.title: See Hear
programme.desc: Yvonne Cobb and Julian Peedle-Calloo learn more about the Covid-19 vaccine rollout. Plus, he closure of units for deaf pupils in mainstream schools. With voiceover

programme.title: Trump Takes On the World
programme.desc: After one of the most shocking presidencies in history, Donald Trump's top advisers and the leaders who clashed with him lift the lid on the critical moments of his foreign policy. In the second programme, Trump turns his attention to the Middle East, where he takes America to the brink of war with Iran, shocks the world by pulling troops out of Syria, and announces breakthrough agreements between Israel and Arab states

programme.title: Bang Goes the Theory
programme.desc: The team investigates the reality of dangerous strains of influenza such as H1N1, or swine flu, which caused widespread panic in late 2009. Liz Bonnin witnesses the making of the vaccines that offer protection during the winter and Maggie Philbin visits a laboratory where healthy volunteers are being deliberately infected to learn how the body is attacked. Jem Stansfield explains flu on a cellular level, showing how it can mutate from the cause of winter snuffles to a disease capable of killing millions

programme.title: The Nine to Five with Stacey Dooley
programme.desc: Stacey takes the work experience teenagers to Sunderland to experience life at one of the country's biggest contact centres. Elliot, Mersadi, Skye, Abbey and Hakeem join the 650 customer experience representatives taking calls on car, home and life insurance

programme.title: The Best Dishes Ever
programme.desc: Ainsley Harriott introduces a selection of old-school recipes. They include Keith Floyd's Irish stew, the Hairy Bikers' meatloaf, James Martin's ultimate bacon butty, a treacle tart from Mary Berry and Paul Hollywood, and a chili con carne from Lorraine Pascale

programme.title: Put Your Money Where Your Mouth Is
programme.desc: Eric Knowles goes head to head with Ochuko Ojiri in Villeneuve-lès-Avignon, France, with Ochuko on the hunt for retro chic, while Eric sniffs out classic French antiques

programme.title: The Great Rift: Africa's Wild Heart
programme.desc: Documentary exploring the grasslands of east Africa, and a line of volcanos stretching from Ethiopia to Tanzania, whose peaks provide a refuge for many wildlife species - which use surprising strategies to survive in such a challenging environment

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: Antique experts Catherine Southon and David Fletcher scour a crowd at Ushaw in Co Durham to find objects to take off to auction. Catherine uncovers a large collection of glassware and stoneware dug up in the north east of England, while David finds an American money box in the shape of a bulldog. Host Paul Martin visits the Oriental Museum at Durham University to find out about the collection of Malcolm McDonald, a British diplomat who was passionate about Asian ceramics

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Nihal Arthanayake, Jake Humphrey, Patsy Kensit and Mae Martin test their skills in a variety of trivia-based games, with the ultimate winner declared on Friday

programme.title: My Unique B&B
programme.desc: Master craftsman Simon Parfett and his team are in Bradford-on-Avon to help single mother Fiona transform her 110-year-old narrowboat into a thriving short-rental business. Faced with an almost empty shell, it looks like being a big job. Before long, Zoe is painting and renovating the interior, while Simon and Ian maximise the space with foldout beds and a copper-fronted bath in the stern

programme.title: Harry Potter: A History of Magic
programme.desc: Documentary exploring the myths, traditions and everyday artefacts that inspired JK Rowling as she crafted the wizarding world depicted in her celebrated Harry Potter series. The British Library is marking the 20th anniversary of the publication of Harry Potter and the Philosopher's Stone with an exhibition showcasing unseen manuscripts, drawings, drafts and personal items from Rowling's private archives, and this programme sheds light on how the objects on display helped to fire the author's imagination over the course of the past two decades. Famous fans offer playful readings that recreate some of the best-loved spells, potion recipes and magical moments from the books, and the programme introduces individuals who have made the magic of the novels a reality, from real-life wand-makers to passionate Quidditch enthusiasts. Narrated by Imelda Staunton

programme.title: Your Garden Made Perfect
programme.desc: Designers Manoj Malde and Tom Massey come up with alternative ideas for the West Sussex garden of wheelchair user Tracey, who can only venture into certain parts of her current space with assistance, and her husband Jake, who dreams of having a low-maintenance plot that he doesn't have to keep looking neat and tidy. Plus, Joel Bird helps a Liverpool couple turn their tiny, dull courtyard into a colourful paradise. Presented by Angela Scanlon

programme.title: Amazing Hotels: Life Beyond the Lobby
programme.desc: Giles Coren and Monica Galetti work in Ashford Castle, Co Mayo. Set in 350 acres, it was founded 800 years ago by the Anglo-Norman De Burgo family, and much later became the family home of the Guinness family. With the help of hotel historian, Fintan, Giles discovers that the castle has had a rollercoaster journey through the centuries, sporadically reaching moments of real jeopardy, only to be saved by a turn of events or new owners. Giles and Monica soon learn that the secret of the castle's success is the people who work here and the hotel's strong, long-running community ties

programme.title: Top of the Pops
programme.desc: A look back at the year when Simply Red, the Housemartins, Sinitta, the Communards and Swing Out Sister hit the charts. It was a time that saw the advent of house music, the big bang in the money markets and political fatigue on the left. How did these real-world events make their mark on the nation's most popular show?

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Analysis of the day's events, presented by Kirsty Wark

programme.title: Being Bridget Jones
programme.desc: Documentary celebrating 25 years of Helen Fielding's hapless creation. The programme examines the ways in which society's attitudes to women have shifted in 25 years, along with the ways in which female writers have told their own stories since Bridget made her debut. Featuring interviews with Helen Fielding, and the friends she based the original characters on, as well as tributes from celebrity fans

programme.title: Forensics: The Real CSI
programme.desc: When a 15-year-old teenager is fatally stabbed in a park in Coventry, the forensics team must identify the murderers through evidence left behind at the crime scene. However, the weapon is nowhere to be found, so they are reliant on just two items - a mobile phone found a short distance from the body and the victim's blood-soaked jacket

programme.title: Celebrity Best Home Cook
programme.desc: The competition reaches the halfway point, where the contestants are challenged by Mary Berry to prepare the ultimate Sunday roast. The only rule is that the meal must involve roasted meat and some form of potatoes - they are otherwise free to do whatever they want. Two cooks compete to produce the best pork crackling, while another adds a Morrocan twist. For the second task, Chris Bavin challenges the cooks to make banana bread without using flour, while the final round involves a classic French dessert

programme.title: Celebrity Best Home Cook
programme.desc: In the quarter-final, the remaining celebrities show off their celebration cakes for Mary Berry's Ultimate Challenge. They also take part in Chris Bavin's all-vegetarian Rustle Up, tasked with making the humble chickpea the star of their dishes, in a bid to avoid Angela Hartnett's dreaded Eliminator. Presented by Claudia Winkleman

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: The Secret Life of Books
programme.desc: Anatomist Alice Roberts explores Mary Shelley's Frankenstein, questioning the main themes of the novel and asking who could have influenced the author's vision of the story. Plus, the presenter travels to the Villa Diodati in Geneva, where Shelley first conceived the idea of Victor Frankenstein's creature, before examining the original manuscript at the Bodleian Library in Oxford

programme.title: The Joy of Painting
programme.desc: American artist Bob Ross paints a larger-than-landscape featuring layers and layers of mountains as far as the eye can see

programme.title: Yes Minister
programme.desc: The visiting head of an African nation turns out to be an old college pal of Hacker - who promptly sets about trying to offload some British-made oil-rigging gear. Satirical comedy set in the world of Westminster, starring Paul Eddington and Nigel Hawthorne

programme.title: The Fall and Rise of Reginald Perrin
programme.desc: Reggie takes his family to a safari park and finds the atmosphere in the car so oppressive that he steps outside to take his chances with the lions. Classic comedy, starring Leonard Rossiter

programme.title: Hattie
programme.desc: Ruth Jones stars as Carry On actress Hattie Jacques in this dramatisation of her secret affair with a younger man in the early 1960s, while still married to actor John Le Mesurier. A love triangle develops between the three and Hattie uses her charm and ingenuity to control escalating family tensions and threats of public exposure - but must ultimately make a decision that puts her at risk of being left on her own. Also starring Robert Bathurst, Aidan Turner and Jeany Spark

programme.title: Mark Lawson Talks To
programme.desc: The journalist interviews the writing duo famous for TV comedy series including The Likely Lads, Porridge, Lovejoy and Auf Wiedersehen, Pet, with their most recent collaboration resulting in the two-part drama Spies of Warsaw. The pair are credited as being among the first to introduce regional dialects and working-class sitcoms to television, and discuss their reputation for rescuing foundering Hollywood scripts

programme.title: Britain and the Sea
programme.desc: David Dimbleby investigates the importance of the sea to trade and to the individual livelihoods of coastal communities. He sails along the west coast of Scotland, starting at Craobh Haven and along the Crinan Canal, a waterway designed by East Lothian engineer John Rennie to take 120 miles off the journey from the west coast to Glasgow and safely avoid the turbulent waters around the Mull of Kintyre. He also climbs aboard the last working sea-going Clyde puffer, where he earns his keep shovelling coal in the steam room, and reflects on the role played by the river in both trade and shipbuilding

programme.title: Man in Room 301
programme.desc: Twelve years after the death of his grandson, Risto Kurtti receives a mysterious letter at his home in Helsinki. Finnish psychological thriller, starring Antti Virmavirta

programme.title: Man in Room 301
programme.desc: Convinced that Leo is Elias Leppo, Risto panics when he learns that his grandson Kalle has gone climbing with him. Unsure about Risto's theory, Eeva calls Seppo to try to find out if Elias is still in Finland. In Finnish

programme.title: The Joy of Painting
programme.desc: American artist Bob Ross paints a larger-than-landscape featuring layers and layers of mountains as far as the eye can see

programme.title: The Secret Life of Books
programme.desc: Anatomist Alice Roberts explores Mary Shelley's Frankenstein, questioning the main themes of the novel and asking who could have influenced the author's vision of the story. Plus, the presenter travels to the Villa Diodati in Geneva, where Shelley first conceived the idea of Victor Frankenstein's creature, before examining the original manuscript at the Bodleian Library in Oxford

programme.title: Coast
programme.desc: Tessa Dunlop and Neil Oliver present an insider guide to southern Wales, from the Severn Bridge to St Davids, as they unearth the coast's wild appeal. Tessa builds on 10 years of Coast stories from the area as she seeks new tales and extreme experiences for the guide, including coastal rowing, exploring why Gower was made Britain's first area of outstanding natural beauty, kayaking at Worm's head, and cockle picking at Penclawdd

programme.title: The Secret Life of Books
programme.desc: Journalist and author Bidisha examines Charlotte Bronte's Jane Eyre, questioning the main themes of the novel and asking what the characterisation of the mad Creole woman in the attic says about they author's views on colonialism. The presenter also travels to the Bronte family's Yorkshire home to find out more about the writer, and examines an original manuscript

programme.title: The Joy of Painting
programme.desc: Bob Ross paints a water scene offset by an abandoned shipping boat hugging a pier and some charming old dockside storage sheds

programme.title: Armada - 12 Days to Save England
programme.desc: Three-part documentary about the sinking of the Spanish Armada, featuring dramatic reconstructions and information gleaned from recently recovered documents. Dan Snow boards a yacht on the English channel to get a sense of the naval battle, revealing how tensions between England and Spain were fuelled by religion, piracy and Elizabeth I's rejection of King Philip II of Spain as a suitor 30 years earlier, and how a missed opportunity could have resulted in disaster. Featuring Anita Dobson as the queen

programme.title: Elizabeth R
programme.desc: Following the death of the Spanish ruler in the Netherlands, the French seek to form a new alliance and lead the rebels to victory. Third part of the award-winning drama chronicling the reign of Elizabeth I, starring Glenda Jackson, Ronald Hines, Robert Hardy, Stephen Murray and Bernard Horsfall

programme.title: Lucy Worsley: Elizabeth I's Battle for God's Music
programme.desc: The presenter investigates the story of choral evensong, which was instigated by Henry VIII during the tumultuous and violent era of the English Reformation. Lucy recounts how Elizabeth I and her two siblings were shaped by the changes their father brought about and how her ideas about religious music found their ultimate expression

programme.title: The Story of Welsh Art
programme.desc: Huw Stephens explores Wales' cultural history, beginning on Anglesey where he steps inside a prehistoric burial chamber to witness Welsh art in its earliest form. He also views an exquisite example of Bronze Age artistry in the shape of a gold cape, found by chance in a field in north Wales and now one of the prize exhibits in the British Museum

programme.title: Storyville
programme.desc: Two journalists pick up the trail of $12.5 billion in cash that was allegedly flown out of Libya in the dead of night just months before Colonel Muammar Gaddafi's death. In South Africa, they discover an eyewitness who seems to know all about the money, but before he can provide them with proof the story takes a sinister twist, the first of many

programme.title: Coast

programme.title: The Secret Life of Books
programme.desc: Documentary series exploring classic works of fiction, continuing with TV script writer Tony Jordan examining Charles Dickens' popular novel Great Expectations. programme.desc: Join four-month-old Annie-Rose and Theo as they discover and explore the world around them through movement. Both children start at home playing with their families. Theo tries pushing and rolling a ball back and forth with his brother and sisters, before playing on his activity mat where he can grab and shake his rattle and kick his legs. Meanwhile, Annie-Rose has a go at grabbing colourful balls and reaching for her soft, cuddly bunny. Mum and dad play a game with her with balloons, as she learns to help them move. Later, Theo goes out with mum to a baby yoga class where he stretches his arms and legs to sing-along songs. Annie-Rose attends a swimming class - singing, swishing and splashing in the pool! When both children get back home, Theo’s mum blows big bubbles in the garden to see how many he can pop, whilst Annie-Rose finishes off on a video call with grandma where she can practice her waving.

programme.desc: Join Emily and Matthew as they discover the world around them at five months old and get stronger every day. Emily has a fun day exploring the great outdoors with her brother, sister, mum and dad. They start their day by playing with a ball, rolling it backwards and forwards between them. Emily even manages to push it away too! Meanwhile, Matthew and his family go to a local play cafe where they have fun in a colourful ball pit. Once Emily's family head out for a walk, they find a beautiful waterfall and stream and decide to have a boat race - but who will come out as the winner? When Matthew arrives at a park, he gets the chance to ride a steam train and find out how they make it go! We then follow Emily as she attends a Baby yoga class with her mum and sister. They learn how to strengthen those baby muscles before singing some songs and bouncing on a giant yoga ball. Matthew finishes his day by going down to the beach to help his siblings build a sandcastle.

programme.title: Andy's Aquatic Adventures
programme.desc: Andy works at Safari World, the biggest and best safari park on the planet, home to animals from all over the globe. The live feed from the bird-cam will be played during Mr Hammonds talk. Jen gets the feed up on the screen to show Andy. The footage shows a young sooty tern flying across the ocean on its fledgling flight. Suddenly, the image turns to static! Mr Hammond really needs that live feed for his talk and Andy realises there’s only one thing for it. It’s time to go on an aquatic adventure! programme.desc: Jay Blades and Dominic Chinea demonstrate DIY tips and tricks that will not break the bank and delve into the BBC archives for advice from the nation's favourite experts. They begin by creating free-standing shelves using scaffolding poles and an old set of drawers

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Alex Jones and Jermaine Jenas present another mix of nationwide reports and live studio-based chat

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Vinny finds himself in a sticky situation when he is cornered in an alley by a thief he had a run-in with before - and this time the man has brought back-up. Meanwhile, Ben and Kheerat anxiously wait at the Vic for Vinny to arrive. Ruby's revelations lead Martin to boiling point with Kush and Stacey

programme.title: Holby City
programme.desc: Hanssen is thrown when Sahira's seriously ill father is admitted to Keller and he tries to avoid treating him. Cameron is backed into a corner when Bobby arrives on the ward threatening to talk. Ange and Dom clash over his decision to sue the hospital as she is forced to choose between her son and her morals

programme.title: Would I Lie to You?
programme.desc: Host Rob Brydon and captains David Mitchell and Lee Mack welcome Johnny Vegas, Alex Jones, Martin Lewis and Anna Maxwell-Martin to the comedy panel show on which the teams reveal extraordinary stories about themselves. But are they telling the truth, or are they making it all up?

programme.title: Why Is Covid Killing People of Colour?
programme.desc: David Harewood sets out to investigate the high Covid-19 death rates in black and minority ethnic patients in the UK, and what they reveal about health inequality in modern Britain. Visiting health experts and doctors at hospitals in areas with high rates of admissions, he discovers the impact of job opportunities and air pollution in deprived communities can leave the residents far more vulnerable, as well as hearing from patients who feel their concerns were disregarded by health professionals

programme.title: Bates Motel
programme.desc: Norman passes out in the classroom after seeing images of his teacher, Miss Watson, tied up, and doctors decide they want to keep him in hospital for observation. Meanwhile, Dylan starts a new job. Starring Freddie Highmore and Vera Farmiga

programme.title: Bates Motel
programme.desc: Norman escapes from Deputy Sheriff Zach Shelby's house with the help of his brother Dylan, who creates a diversion - but the girl he found in the basement is left behind. He tells his mother what has happened but she does not believe him, saying that he must sometimes be seeing things that are not there

programme.desc: Lucy Alexander, Martin Roberts and Dion Dublin explore properties about to be auctioned in South Norwood, Croydon, Alvaston in Derby and Cumbria

programme.title: For Love or Money
programme.desc: A woman who was drawn into a psychologically abusive relationship and conned into handing over more than £60,000 of her life savings

programme.title: Ill Gotten Gains
programme.desc: The team are in demand covering drug raids across Lancashire and beyond. Plus, how a woman helped police get the better of a fraudster in Durham

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Charlie Ross is joined by experts Thomas Plant and Nick Hall at Nottingham racecourse, and finds out how black and immigrant miners helped fuel Nottinghamshire

programme.title: BBC Newsline; Weather
programme.desc: Local news worth watching from across Northern Ireland

programme.title: Doctors
programme.desc: After some cajoling by Bear, Sid finally gets back in the game, while Karen and Luca patch things up and Jimmi helps an ex-offender with a violent past

programme.title: Shakespeare & Hathaway - Private Investigators
programme.desc: Frank and Lu's puzzle-solving skills are put to the test when they investigate a mysterious car accident which killed a young mother

programme.title: Escape to the Perfect Town
programme.desc: Sonali Shah searches for homes in Totnes in Devon for a buyer with a budget of £320,000, who wants to trade her hectic life in London for the slower pace of a market town

programme.title: Ready Steady Cook
programme.desc: Sisters Anne and Janet take part in the culinary contest, teaming up with chefs Anna Haugh and Akis Petretzikis. Rylan Clark-Neal hosts

programme.title: The Bidding Room
programme.desc: The dealers compete to buy a model Spitfire, a pair of gilt cherubs, two ancient stone Celtic heads and a retro pub sign from a West Midlands brewery. Presented by Nigel Havers

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Magazine show, with stories of interest from around the UK, plus celebrity guests in the studio. Presented by Alex Jones and Jermaine Jenas

programme.title: Barra on the Foyle
programme.desc: Barra Best makes a journey across the 4000 square kilometres of the Foyle river network, crisscrossing his way across the many rivers and streams as he follows its route across three counties, Donegal, Tyrone and Londonderry, to Lough Foyle. Along the way, he meets with some of those who work, live and play along its banks, exploring places and landscapes rarely seen on our television screens and all brought to life with some stunning aerial footage. In the first episode, Barra uncovers the mystery of the Beaghmore Stone Circles, before going white water rafting on the River Strule

programme.title: MasterChef
programme.desc: Five more contenders compete for a place in the quarter finals. In the first round, they must prepare their signature dish, while the second sees them preparing a meal involving their favourite ingredient. One contestant will be eliminated in these two rounds, leaving the others to go through to the final challenge - preparing a two-course meal for judges Gregg Wallace and John Torode and past champions Saliha Mahmood Ahmed and Kenny Tutt

programme.title: Gordon Ramsay's Bank Balance
programme.desc: Pairs of contestants answer a series of questions in order to place different sized stacks of gold bars on a volatile Balance Board and the more bars they build up, the more they can win to a jackpot of £100,000. However, the more bars that accumulate, the greater the chance they will fall down requiring players to demonstrate both knowledge and nerve to triumph in a game show that's as unpredictable in nature as its host

programme.title: Nolan Live
programme.desc: Stephen Nolan probes the issues that matter to the people of Northern Ireland, focusing on the stories of the week and the people making the headlines in lively debates, with viewers sending in their questions via text, email and tweet

programme.title: Match of the Day
programme.desc: Gary Lineker presents highlights of the latest Premier League fixtures, featuring Burnley v Leicester City at Turf Moor, Crystal Palace v Manchester United at Selhurst Park and Sheffield United v Aston Villa at Bramall Lane. Plus, a look back at last night's contest between Manchester City and Wolverhampton Wanderers

programme.title: A Question of Sport
programme.desc: Captains Matt Dawson and Phil Tufnell are joined by former world champion boxer Anthony Crolla, Commonwealth silver medallist high jumper Morgan Lake, former British tennis number one Greg Rusedski and Olympic medal-winning swimmer Steve Parry. Sue Barker hosts

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Naga Munchetty and Charlie Stayt present a round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Morning Live
programme.desc: Kym Marsh and Gethin Jones host the weekday magazine show featuring a mixture of topical chat and expert advice

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin Roberts heads to Newport in south Wales to see a plot of land with no planning permission, while Jacqui Joseph is in Eastleigh, Hampshire, to visit a three-bed mid-terrace, and Dion Dublin visits a one-bedroom bungalow in Lichfield, Staffordshire, that requires change of use to make it a residential dwelling again

programme.title: For Love or Money
programme.desc: Kym Marsh and Ashley John-Baptiste meet a woman whose online friend tried to gain her trust and affection to fleece her out of thousands of pounds

programme.title: Ill Gotten Gains
programme.desc: Police in Belfast show off a collection of designer goods they seized from a suspected paramilitary drug dealer, and a man who stole from his girlfriend has his assets sold off

programme.title: Bargain Hunt

programme.title: BBC Newsline; Weather
programme.desc: Local news worth watching from across Northern Ireland

programme.title: Doctors
programme.desc: Luca is backed into a corner when his past comes back to haunt him, Bear and Sid go on a double date, and Karen meets a mother who is desperate to save her sick daughter

programme.title: Shakespeare & Hathaway - Private Investigators
programme.desc: Frank and Lu are hired to locate a photograph stolen from a local pub, whose owner is in the middle of a feud with another local family - only for events to take a deadly turn

programme.title: Escape to the Perfect Town
programme.desc: Steve Brown takes a couple round the West Yorkshire market town of Wetherby, showing them properties for their £550,000 budget and also exploring the local cafes and horticulture

programme.title: Ready Steady Cook
programme.desc: Engaged couple Nick and Donnie compete with the help of chefs Jeremy Pang and Anna Haugh, seeing who can come up with the best meal against the clock. Hosted by Rylan Clark-Neal

programme.title: The Bidding Room
programme.desc: Sellers try to get a deal for a painting by early 20th-century artist William Russell Flint, a tin rocket toy, a set of retro formica furniture, and a carved Japanese walking stick

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman

programme.title: The One Show

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Kat feels uneasy when Phil suggests that next time they should meet up in a hotel, worried that things are getting a little too serious for her liking. Lily confronts Ruby about the miscarriage, telling her it was not Stacey's fault. Denise suggests asking Jack for help with the family's problems - but Chelsea does not like the thought of him sniffing around - and Keegan is not happy to hear about Tiffany and Dotty's big money-making plan

programme.title: Pooch Perfect
programme.desc: Sheridan Smith presents the final of the contestant, which sees the three remaining contestants aiming to become top dog-groomer - and win themselves the Golden Stanley trophy to prove it. All they have to do is execute one of the toughest challenges in the industry - a continental clip on an elegant standard poodle. As if that were not enough, they have to guess a breed of dog while blindfolded, before one last dogwalk parade. Who will impress judges Verity Hardcastle and Colin Taylor the most?

programme.title: Gordon Ramsay's Bank Balance
programme.desc: Contestant pairs Latia and Rose, and Chris and Luke answer a series of questions to place different sized stacks of gold bars on a volatile Balance Board - and the more bars they build up, the more they can win to a jackpot of £100,000. However, the higher they go, the greater the chance of falling, requiring players to demonstrate both knowledge and nerve

programme.desc: Anton and Alisha are now parents to a little baby girl. They take her to meet the children in the nursery, who are amazed at how small she is and form some interesting ideas

programme.title: Sruth
programme.desc: Music show exploring the deep connections that link Scottish and Irish music. Guitarist Innes White performs with Ríoghnach Connolly and Irish fiddle player Méabh Smyth meets Duncan Chisholm

programme.title: Seòid a' Chidsin - The Kitchen Coves
programme.desc: The lads enter the Barra and Vatersay Potato Competition

programme.title: Alba Today
programme.desc: BBC Alba joins Radio nan Gàidheal, the BBC's Gaelic language radio service

programme.title: Alba Today
programme.desc: BBC Alba joins Radio nan Gàidheal, the BBC's Gaelic language radio service

programme.title: Ceitidh Morag (Katie Morag)
programme.desc: Spring arrives, and the youngster is very fond of the new lambs

programme.title: 'S E Iasg a Th'Annam (I'm a Fish)
programme.desc: The Blue Dragon Fish tells us how she is a Sea Slug and that she loves to float on the surface of the ocean

programme.title: Shane an Chef
programme.desc: Shane gets an idea from Mama Polenta for something different for the menu

programme.title: Abadas
programme.desc: The friends are sent in search of a rocking horse

programme.title: Rita is Crogall
programme.desc: Rita loves to ride her bicycle round the park, but it’s not that easy without stabilisers

programme.title: Stòiridh
programme.desc: Catriona MacPhee reads A Bhith nam Leòmhann written and illustrated by Emma Carlisle and translated into Gaelic by Jo Macdonald

programme.title: Donnie Murdo (Danger Mouse)
programme.desc: One of DM's flying kicks damages the holodeck, and he and Penfold wake to find they have been in a three-year dream, that they aren't secret agents and work in IT instead

programme.title: Ronia, Nighean a' Mheirlich/Ronja, the Robber's Daughter
programme.desc: Ronia and Biorc have promised one another to be like sister and brother, but when she is ill and stuck indoors, Ronia feels worried that she isn’t able to see Biorc

programme.title: Geamaichean Gorach
programme.desc: Olaidh Orains and Coinneachd Clogaid compete as always, but Olaidh has it in the bag

programme.title: Port Pàipeir
programme.desc: Matilda has the power to excel in business, being able to make calculations and predict market behaviour. Tropecio Ferguson requests her help for his new 'harmless' business

programme.title: Turas a' Bhradain
programme.desc: Neen MacKay explores Scotland's fishing rivers, focusing on the nation's hydro river systems, which are respected the world over

programme.title: Speaking Our Language
programme.desc: An opportunity to learn Gaelic. Presented by Rhoda MacDonald

programme.title: Earrann Eachdraidh (History Shorts)
programme.desc: Documentary about caring individuals, including nurses

programme.title: An Là (News)
programme.desc: Local, national and international news and information from BBC ALBA

programme.title: Eòrpa
programme.desc: The use of new national materials to teach personal education in the Western Isles, which has been the focus of much debate among, parents, church leaders and councillors

programme.title: Sruth
programme.desc: Pàdruig Morrison from Grimsay in the Western Isles performs with Bríd Harper, the Co Donegal musician and one of the finest Irish traditional fiddlers of her generation

programme.title: Iùl a' Chiùil (The Musical Path)
programme.desc: Exploring innovation in Irish and Scottish traditional music, charting the shifting sands between tradition and innovation over the past half -century and featuring some of the best new, cutting-edge acts including Zoe Conway and John McIntyre; Chris Stout and Catriona Mackay, and Rónán Ó Snodaigh

programme.title: Eileanan na Banrighe/Islands of the Queen
programme.desc: Documentary exploring the diversity of the islands of the Commonwealth. From weathered stone huts in the in upland moors to golden Caribbean beaches, the series shows the isles in all their contrast and glory

programme.title: FilmG
programme.desc: Filmed and edited by students from the Nicolson Institute in Stornoway during their 2019 summer holidays, An-Dràsta! presents a local perspective on a global crisis, examining how the climate emergency is affecting the Outer Hebrides

programme.title: Na Millenni-Gaels
programme.desc: Anton and Alisha are now parents to a little baby girl. They take her to meet the children in the nursery, who are amazed at how small she is and form some interesting ideas

programme.title: Alba Today
programme.desc: BBC Alba joins Radio nan Gàidheal, the BBC's Gaelic language radio service

programme.title: Good Morning Britain
programme.desc: Morning magazine featuring a lively mix of news and current affairs, plus health, entertainment and lifestyle features

programme.title: Lorraine
programme.desc: Entertainment, current affairs and fashion news, as well as showbiz stories and celebrity gossip. Presented by Lorraine Kelly

programme.title: This Morning
programme.desc: Outlander star Sam Heughan talks about new thriller film SAS: Red Notice

programme.title: Loose Women
programme.desc: More celebrity interviews and topical debate from a female perspective

programme.title: Judge Rinder
programme.desc: Cameras follow criminal barrister Robert Rinder as he takes on real-life cases in a studio courtroom

programme.title: Tenable
programme.desc: Quiz hosted by Warwick Davis in which a team of five members of a Cardiff family answers questions about top 10 lists from the realms of pop culture and general knowledge, then tries to score a perfect 10 in the final round to take home the prize money

programme.title: Tipping Point
programme.desc: Ben Shephard hosts the quiz in which contestants answer questions to win turns on an arcade-style machine. Dropping tokens down a choice of four chutes, they hope to knock piles of them off a moving shelf - and the more they collect, the greater the prize fund. The player who has won the least amount is then eliminated, and the last one standing competes for a £10,000 jackpot

programme.title: The Chase
programme.desc: Bradley Walsh presents as contestants Julia, Emma, Paul and Michael pit their wits against one of the ruthless Chasers in the hope of winning a potential prize pot worth thousands of pounds. They work as a team and play strategically to answer general knowledge questions against the clock and race down the game board to the exit without being caught

programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: Laurel's worried for Gabby, Al is angry and Mandy makes a proposal

programme.title: For the Love of Britain
programme.desc: Julie Walters presents a guide to some of the most beautiful sights in the Dorset countryside. Sheila Hancock revisits some childhood memories at the Dancing Ledge rock pools, while Martin Kemp visits the fishing village of Mudeford and heads out to sea for a very special catch. Liz Bonnin checks in at Portland Bill, home to millions of migrating birds; and Ben Fogle gets the most spectacular sky-high view of the Jurassic coast

programme.title: Million Pound Pawn
programme.desc: A Sheffield pawnbroker takes a helicopter ride to the biggest deal of his career - and sees another chance to make a profit when a customer brings in a Rolex she bought for £25 in a car boot sale. John Freeze faces a tough choice when offered a collection of 20 designer handbags, knowing that the fickle nature of fashion can make their value highly changeable. Ray takes delivery of a bespoke, hand-made Rolls Royce - but with the wedding industry it was made for at a standstill due to Covid-19, buyers may be in short supply

programme.title: Marcella
programme.desc: The detective cuts ties with her old life as she engineers to become the centre of the Maguire family with Finn. But her conscience continues to threaten her. Rory rejects Finn's plan to take over the company from their mother, only to find himself forced into submission. Eddie is ordered to kill Stacey, who poses a threat to the family

programme.title: ITV News at Ten
programme.desc: A fresh perspective on the major stories of the day, featuring national and international news, with reports and analysis from ITV's correspondents

programme.title: Marcella
programme.desc: The fault lines Marcella has set in motion within the family reach crisis point, when she wakes up from a fugue in Finn's car to find something terrible in the boot. However, when she asks Frank for help with extraction, he does not respond. Turning to Stacey for help, she promises to get both of them and the baby safely away from the Maguire family

programme.title: Made in Britain
programme.desc: A look behind the scenes at British brands, including an insight into how Yorkshire puddings are made on a huge scale, and life inside Britain's last remaining pencil factory. Plus, how one man's quest to make the perfect present for his dad ended with him producing handmade globes that are now in demand around the world. Narrated by Ricky Tomlinson

programme.title: The Bay
programme.desc: As a confession brings the team closer to the truth, Lisa identifies a suspect, but conflicting accounts leave her questioning who to believe. With the complexities of the case proving difficult to crack, the stakes are raised as the race to bring those responsible to justice takes an unexpected turn, and Lisa is faced with a hard decision to protect the people she loves

programme.desc: Short bulletin rounding up the day's showbusiness stories

programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: Paul and Mandy are excited, Lydia is uneasy and Al is smug

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Alya finds Yasmeen in full meltdown, convinced she is going back to prison. Asha and Aadi plan Dev's surprise birthday party, but Asha is disappointed when Nina says she can't come. Jenny is flattered when Neil, the guy who flirted with her in the bistro, calls at the Rovers and proceeds to flirt with her again

programme.title: Best Walks with a View with Julia Bradbury
programme.desc: The expert walker visits the Isle of Man, where her favourite island walk has it all - an ancient Manx river glen, the world's biggest mining waterwheel and a climb that culminates with spectacular views of England, Scotland and Ireland. With steam trains and electric trams on the TT island famous for Thomas the Tank Engine, her latest trek promises to be action-packed

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Alya and Tim force their way in and are horrified to find Yasmeen collapsed on the floor, struggling to breathe. Jenny orders Daisy to apologise to Neil for leading him on, while Nina admits to Aadi that it just felt too soon to be attending family parties, but the last thing she wants to do is hurt Asha

programme.title: Fergie's Killer Dresser: The Jane Andrews Story
programme.desc: The story of Jane Andrews, the former royal dresser who was convicted of murdering her boyfriend Tom Cressman in 2001, and released on parole 14 years later. The film uses interviews with Jane's family and psychiatrist as well as key players in the police investigation and prosecution to ask whether lingering doubts about the case are justified. Could it have been a case of manslaughter not murder? Or are the victim's family and the prosecution right to consider her 'a danger to all men?'

programme.title: ITV News at Ten
programme.desc: A fresh perspective on the major stories of the day, featuring national and international news, with reports and analysis from ITV's correspondents

programme.title: Peston
programme.desc: Political magazine show presented by Robert Peston, featuring major interviews with MPs, topical guests and cultural figures. Including updates from social media taking in perspectives from viewers, experts and key players throughout the programme

programme.title: Made in Britain
programme.desc: A look at how the how the hovercraft inspired the invention of one of Britain's best-selling lawnmowers, an insight into the making of a suitcase beloved by royalty, Arctic explorers and Hollywood celebrities. Plus, what it takes to craft a spoon fit for the Downing Street dinner table, and the bangers that turned the sausage status-quo on its head. Ricky Tomlinson narrates

programme.title: The Chase
programme.desc: Bradley Walsh presents as contestants Jill, Beorn, Benny and Annie pit their wits against the Chaser. Will it be a case of winner takes it all for the quartet or will they reach their waterloo against the quiz genius?

programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: Al fires Priya, Charity forms a plan and Lydia tries to bury her feelings

programme.title: Tonight
programme.desc: Julie Etchingham asks whether the United Kingdom is on the brink of a break-up. Using polls of English, Scottish, Welsh and Northern Irish voters, the programme discovers how people feel about the union. Julia examines they way Scots are being driven toward independence, and why continuing issues around Brexit are making people in Northern Ireland question their role in the UK

programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: Charity is in trouble, Cain gets emotional and Ellis and Priya get close

programme.title: The Martin Lewis Money Show: Live
programme.desc: Fresh from the Government's budget announcements, Martin takes a look at what it all means for family finances. Plus, more News You Can Use and the latest information on savings and consumer rights

programme.title: Piers Morgan's Life Stories
programme.desc: Piers chats to actor and director Rupert Everett, who reflects on the caustic comments he has made about several co-stars, including Madonna and Julia Roberts. He also talks about the promiscuous life he led in the 1970s and 1980s, the impact of the HIV/Aids crisis, and discusses his romantic relationships with women, including TV presenter Paula Yates

programme.title: ITV News at Ten
programme.desc: A fresh perspective on the major stories of the day, featuring national and international news, with reports and analysis from ITV's correspondents

programme.title: 56 Up
programme.desc: The concluding part of the 2012 update in the landmark documentary series, following the stories of a group of participants whose lives have been chronicled since 1964. Having last spoken to the cameras in 2005, the participants, who are now 56, talk to Michael Apted about the highs and lows of getting older, reveal whether they have achieved their ambitions and reflect on their appearances in previous instalments of the programme

programme.title: Made in Britain
programme.desc: The master baker who turns out a traditional regional delicacy with a Greek twist and the craftsmen who turn raw hides into handcrafted saddles

programme.desc: Short bulletin rounding up the day's showbusiness stories

programme.desc: A team of colleagues from the British Transport Police compete in the game show, taking it in turns to find the answers to top 10 lists, before trying to score a perfect 10 in the final round. Hosted by Warwick Davis

programme.title: Tipping Point
programme.desc: Ben Shephard hosts the quiz in which contestants answer questions to win turns on an arcade-style machine. Dropping tokens down a choice of four chutes, they hope to knock piles of them off a moving shelf - and the more they collect, the greater the prize fund. The player who has won the least amount is then eliminated, and the last one standing competes for a £10,000 jackpot

programme.title: The Chase
programme.desc: Bradley Walsh presents as contestants Jill, Beorn, Benny and Annie pit their wits against the Chaser. Will it be a case of winner takes it all for the quartet or will they reach their waterloo against the quiz genius?

programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: Al fires Priya, Charity forms a plan and Lydia tries to bury her feelings

programme.title: Tonight
programme.desc: Julie Etchingham asks whether the United Kingdom is on the brink of a break-up. Using polls of English, Scottish, Welsh and Northern Irish voters, the programme discovers how people feel about the union. Julia examines they way Scots are being driven toward independence, and why continuing issues around Brexit are making people in Northern Ireland question their role in the UK

programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: Charity is in trouble, Cain gets emotional and Ellis and Priya get close

programme.title: The Martin Lewis Money Show: Live
programme.desc: Fresh from the Government's budget announcements, Martin takes a look at what it all means for family finances. Plus, more News You Can Use and the latest information on savings and consumer rights

programme.title: Piers Morgan's Life Stories
programme.desc: Piers chats to actor and director Rupert Everett, who reflects on the caustic comments he has made about several co-stars, including Madonna and Julia Roberts. He also talks about the promiscuous life he led in the 1970s and 1980s, the impact of the HIV/Aids crisis, and discusses his romantic relationships with women, including TV presenter Paula Yates

programme.title: ITV News at Ten
programme.desc: A fresh perspective on the major stories of the day, featuring national and international news, with reports and analysis from ITV's correspondents

programme.title: 56 Up
programme.desc: The concluding part of the 2012 update in the landmark documentary series, following the stories of a group of participants whose lives have been chronicled since 1964. Having last spoken to the cameras in 2005, the participants, who are now 56, talk to Michael Apted about the highs and lows of getting older, reveal whether they have achieved their ambitions and reflect on their appearances in previous instalments of the programme

programme.title: Made in Britain
programme.desc: The master baker who turns out a traditional regional delicacy with a Greek twist and the craftsmen who turn raw hides into handcrafted saddles

programme.title: You've Been Framed!
programme.desc: Harry Hill narrates a comical selection of mishaps filmed by viewers. Andy Murray's mum toughens up her son by using him for target practice, viewers learn what the Mastermind chair does on its day off, and Peter Stringfellow's jumbo 'tablet' gives someone a black eye. The show also features a helicopter for hamsters, a goat who thinks that he is a butler, and Harry is reunited with the daughter that he never knew

programme.title: You've Been Framed!
programme.desc: Surreal comedy from the mind of Harry Hill, inspired by viewers' clips, featuring a person resembling Steven Spielberg who finds an explosive cure for his constipation, and a Clare Balding lookalike who marries a horse. Plus, Steve and his friends play a prank on Roger, who decides to terrorise them as he seeks vengeance

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: A young woman asks Poirot to investigate her father's murder - a 14-year-old crime for which her mother was found guilty and executed. The detective agrees to investigate, but warns his client that he may discover things she does not want to hear. David Suchet stars, with Rachael Stirling, Aidan Gillen, Aimee Mullens, Toby Stephens, Gemma Jones, Patrick Malahide and Annette Badland

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Tragedy casts a dark shadow over the idyllic landscape of Aidensfield when Nick receives an anonymous tip-off about a local shopkeeper's unsavoury antics. programme.desc: Ken is disgusted when he finds out Alec's plan. During an argument, Nick reveals to Gail the Mallets' deal with Zoe over the baby

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Gail accuses Leanne of spreading lies about the Malletts. Ken reluctantly agrees to join Alec's escort agency, and Jon surprises Deirdre by asking her to marry him

programme.title: Inspector Morse
programme.desc: First-ever episode of the popular crime drama, starring John Thaw as author Colin Dexter's Oxford detective who treats his cases as intellectual challenges. In this mystery, Morse is introduced to trusty Sergeant Lewis (Kevin Whately) and takes it upon himself to investigate the apparent suicide of a lady friend. Co-starring Gemma Jones, Patrick Troughton, James Grout and Peter Woodthorpe

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: The detective's friend Sir Charles Cartwright (guest star Martin Shaw) hosts a cocktail party at his home in Cornwall, where an amiable local priest chokes to death on his drink. There seems to be no motive for murder, and no trace of poison found in the victim's glass, but weeks later, when Sir Charles's doctor friend Sir Bartholomew Strange dies in similar circumstances, he and Poirot team up to investigate. Drama, with Art Malik, Jane Asher and Anna Carteret, and David Suchet in the title role

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: A disused abbey that is believed to be cursed is about to be turned into a pub, a decision that has angered many superstitious locals - not to mention the real ale society. The day after the launch night, the curse appears to have claimed a victim - a body is found boiled to death in a kettle of beer, in a manner very similar to an infamous execution on the site in the 16th century. Elaine Paige guest stars, while Annette Badland joins the cast as new pathologist Fleur Perkins

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: English country squire Marcus Waverly receives a series of anonymous letters threatening to kidnap his son Johnnie, but the case is dismissed by Scotland Yard - prompting Poirot to open his own investigation. Drama, starring David Suchet and Geoffrey Bateman

programme.title: Scott & Bailey
programme.desc: Part one of two. The badly burned body of a disabled man turns up in a remote part of Manchester and a few days later another corpse is discovered. The detectives start to realise the victims were not just murdered, but tortured too. They make an arrest, but it becomes clear their investigation has just scratched the surface. Meanwhile, Janet throws her husband out and Rachel's estranged brother turns up on her doorstep. With Lisa Riley and Pippa Haywood

programme.title: A Touch of Frost
programme.desc: The severed arm of a missing environmental health inspector is washed up on the shores of the Humber and Frost's investigation leads him to a local meat factory. Unfortunately, his new lodger's annoying dog keeps distracting him from the case. Starring David Jason, Bruce Alexander, Emma Kay, Niall O'Brien and Tom Georgeson

programme.desc: The detective's friend Sir Charles Cartwright (guest star Martin Shaw) hosts a cocktail party at his home in Cornwall, where an amiable local priest chokes to death on his drink.
